About
Casting guide

How to Use I Ching Coins for a Simple Reading

A coin reading turns a question into six lines. The value is not in surrendering judgment, but in slowing down enough to see the situation clearly.

Start with a clear question

Good questions are specific but not desperate. Ask what to notice, what pattern is forming, or what kind of response is wiser. Avoid questions that demand a guaranteed prediction.

Cast six lines from bottom to top

Traditional coin methods build the hexagram one line at a time. Record each line carefully. The bottom line comes first, which is different from how many beginners expect to read a symbol.

Read the primary and relating hexagram

The primary hexagram describes the current pattern. Changing lines show movement. The relating hexagram suggests where the situation may be going if that movement continues.

Use the reading as a mirror

The I Ching should not replace professional advice or personal responsibility. Treat it as a structured reflection method with cultural depth.

Try the tool

Cast a quick reading with the local tool.

Open tool
